title: indulgent canadian gobo-burdock stew
description: this hearty stew combines the earthy flavors of gobo and burdock with a rich, silky sauce. a shojin-friendly, one-pot dish perfect for a cold canadian winter's night.
instructions: heat the sesame oil in a large pot over medium-low heat.
add the sliced gobo and burdock and cook, stirring occasionally, until they begin to soften, about 10 minutes.
add the red wine and bring to a simmer, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
add the vegetable broth and kombu seaweed, and bring to a boil.
reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.
remove the kombu seaweed and discard.
stir in the tapioca starch and cook for an additional 5 minutes, or until the stew has thickened slightly.
season with black pepper to taste.
serve hot, garnished with chopped green onions or sesame seeds if desired.
